title: 5 ORS User Management
body_text:
5	ORS User Management
5.1	ORS User Types
There are four ORS user types: Research Users, Return Users, Upload Users, and Script Users.
Research Users are authorized to
â
search, view, print, and save images of cleared cheques (including suspected duplicates);
â
search, view, print, and save images of adjustments; and
â
search, view, print, and save images of deposit slips and cheques on deposit.
Return Users have all the permissions of Research Users and are authorized to
â
search, view, print, and save AFT transactions;
â
return AFT transactions;
â
create AFT Notice of Change transactions;
â
return dishonoured cheques;
â
reinstate incorrect information on cheques, including the Transit Number, account number, and serial (cheque) number; and
â
view and print daily ORS reports that summarize cheque and AFT returns entered in ORS.
Note:	Only AFT-activated financial institutions can view, print, save, and return AFT transactions.
Upload Users have all the permissions of the Return User and are authorized to manually upload Canadian Cheque Return files (.RI), USD Cheque Return files (.RIU), AFT Return files (.RTN), and AFT Notice of Change files (.NOC) to ORS.
Script Users are permitted to upload Canadian Cheque Return files (.RI), USD Cheque Return files (.RIU), AFT Return Files (.RTN), and AFT Notice of Change files (.NOC) manually to ORS or via Central 1âs FTP server.
The following table summarizes the ORS functions to which each user type has access.

* This function is only available if your financial institution is set up for this ORS service.
**Financial institutions that require script or manual file uploads must coordinate file tests with Central 1. Once the Online Return System (ORS) Application, Form 2193, is submitted, Central 1 will contact you to coordinate testing.
5.2	Creating ORS Users
Authorized users access ORS via password login to the Client Centre. Each ORS user must be set up as a Client Centre User. Since ORS doesnât have an independent user management component, all ORS set-up information is entered on User Management. See the User Management Part for procedures if your financial institution is using User Management.
5.3	Modifying ORS Users
Since ORS doesnât have an independent user management component, changes to ORS users are entered on User Management. See the User Management Part for procedures on modifying ORS users.
5.4	Deleting ORS Users
Since ORS doesnât have an independent user management component, access to ORS is removed via the individualâs Client Centre user profile.
Caution
:	See the User Management Part for procedures on deleting access to ORS or deleting a Client Centre User.
END
